fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Mississippi Occupational Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a occupational therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Mississippi - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: occupational therapist license reciprocity mississippi, how to transfer occupational therapist license to mississippi, occupational therapist compact mississippi, mississippi occupational therapist endorsement application

board.name: Mississippi Board of Occupational Therapy
board.mailing_address:
Professional Licensure
Mississippi State Department of Health
P.O. Box 1700
Jackson, MS 39215-1700

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT) examination

license_types.title: Occupational Therapist Registered (OTR)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice occupational therapy independently in Mississippi.

license_types.title: Certified Occupational Therapy Assistant (COTA)
license_types.description: Requires an associate degree and practicing under the supervision of a licensed OTR.

steps.title: Graduate from an Accredited Program & Complete Fieldwork
steps.description: Complete an occupational therapy educational program accredited by the Accreditation Council for Occupational Therapy Education (ACOTE) and fulfill all supervised fieldwork requirements.

steps.title: Pass the NBCOT Examination
steps.description: Successfully pass the National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT) OTR examination.

steps.title: Apply Online via the MSDH Online Licensure Portal
steps.description: Access the MSDH Online Licensure Portal, register as a new applicant, complete the online application for initial licensure, and pay the required application fee.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documentation
steps.description: Arrange for your official academic transcript and official NBCOT score report to be sent directly from the issuing institutions to the Mississippi State Department of Health.

steps.title: Undergo Background Check
steps.description: Complete any required background checks as instructed during the application process within the MSDH Online Licensure Portal.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBCOT exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBCOT exam. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Occupational therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current NBCOT certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Mississippi OT license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around  to . Check the specific fee breakdown.

faqs.question: Is NBCOT certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBCOT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across all states.

faqs.question: Does Mississippi have reciprocity for occupational therapists?
faqs.answer: Mississippi has enacted the OT Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, occupational therapists still apply by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my occupational therapist license to Mississippi?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Mississippi while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Occupational therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current NBCOT certification.

hero.title: Become a Occupational Therapist in Mississippi
hero.dek: To apply for an Occupational Therapist license in Mississippi, use the MSDH Online Licensure Portal.

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the MSDH Online Licensure Portal, select 'Initial Application,' and then click 'New Applicant: Register Here' to create an account using your Social Security Number, Date of Birth, Last Name, and Email.
